text:
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

PATIENT: Robert Delacroix
MRN: [REDACTED]
DOB: [REDACTED]
SEX: Male
ORDERING PHYSICIAN: [Urology/Oncology Attending]
PATHOLOGIST: [Attending Pathologist]

------------------------------------------------------------------------
SPECIMEN INFORMATION
------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specimen ID: SP-[REDACTED]-A through SP-[REDACTED]-L (12 separate containers)
Procedure Type: Transrectal Ultrasound (TRUS)-Guided Prostate Needle Biopsy, 12-core systematic sampling
Clinical Indication: Elevated serum PSA 87.4 ng/mL; abnormal DRE (firm, nodular bilateral lobes); rule out prostate carcinoma
Clinical History: 68-year-old male. No prior prostate biopsy on record. Family history of prostate cancer (father). Former smoker.

Containers submitted:
  A - Right base
  B - Right mid
  C - Right mid-lateral
  D - Right lateral
  E - Left base
  F - Left mid
  G - Left mid-lateral
  H - Left lateral
  I - Right apex
  J - Right apex lateral
  K - Left apex
  L - Left apex lateral

------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION
------------------------------------------------------------------------

Received are twelve (12) individually labeled containers (A through L), each containing one to two tan-white, cylindrical core needle biopsy fragments in formalin. Cores range from 1.3 to 1.8 cm in length and 0.1 to 0.2 cm in diameter. All cores are entirely submitted after inking and serial sectioning at 3 mm intervals. Multiple levels are prepared from each block and stained with hematoxylin and eosin (H&E). Additional unstained sections are reserved for possible immunohistochemical studies.

Total tissue submitted: 12 cores, entirely processed.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION
------------------------------------------------------------------------

CORES A-B (Right base, 2 cores examined):
Both cores demonstrate extensive replacement by infiltrating adenocarcinoma. The tumor is composed predominantly of poorly-formed glands and individual cells with marked nuclear enlargement, prominent macronucleoli, and amphophilic to pale cytoplasm. A minor component shows large cribriform structures with central necrosis (comedonecrosis), consistent with Gleason pattern 5. The dominant growth pattern is Gleason pattern 4 (poorly-formed/fused glands). Estimated tumor involvement: approximately 90-100% of core A; 70-80% of core B. Perineural invasion is identified in core A (two separate foci). Lymphovascular space invasion is not identified. No benign prostate tissue is present in core A. A rim of compressed stromal tissue is present at the periphery of core B.

Gleason score (cores A-B): 4+5=9, Grade Group 5

CORES C-D (Right mid and right mid-lateral, 2 cores examined):
Infiltrating adenocarcinoma is present throughout both cores. Glandular architecture ranges from poorly-formed acini (Gleason pattern 4) to solid nests and single cells with prominent nucleoli (Gleason pattern 5). Sheets of cells with necrosis are noted. Estimated tumor involvement: approximately 80% of core C; 70% of core D. Perineural invasion present in core C (one focus). No fibromuscular stroma free of tumor identified.

Gleason score (cores C-D): 4+5=9, Grade Group 5

CORES E-F (Left base and left mid, 2 cores examined):
Infiltrating adenocarcinoma is present. Tumor demonstrates predominantly Gleason pattern 4 morphology: ill-defined glands, fused small acini, and cribriform architecture without central necrosis. A minor component of single infiltrating cells is consistent with Gleason pattern 4 (not reaching threshold for pattern 5). Estimated tumor involvement: approximately 60-70% of core E; 50-60% of core F. Perineural invasion absent. Benign prostatic glands, some with partial atrophy, are identified at the edge of both cores.

Gleason score (cores E-F): 4+4=8, Grade Group 4

CORES G-H (Left mid-lateral and left lateral, 2 cores examined):
Infiltrating adenocarcinoma is present. Glandular architecture similar to cores E-F, predominantly Gleason pattern 4 with ill-defined and fused acini. Occasional poorly-formed glands are seen at the infiltrating front. Estimated tumor involvement: approximately 40-50% of core G; 35-45% of core H. Perineural invasion is identified in core H (one small caliber nerve). Scattered benign glands with luminal secretions present at core edges.

Gleason score (cores G-H): 4+4=8, Grade Group 4

CORES I-J (Right apex and right apex lateral, 2 cores examined):
Infiltrating adenocarcinoma present. Growth pattern shows Gleason pattern 4 (cribriform and poorly-formed glands) admixed with Gleason pattern 5 (single cells, cords). Estimated tumor involvement: approximately 80% of core I; 65-70% of core J. Perineural invasion present in core I. Extraprostatic extension cannot be evaluated on needle core material; however, periprostatic adipose tissue is not sampled.

Gleason score (cores I-J): 4+5=9, Grade Group 5

CORES K-L (Left apex and left apex lateral, 2 cores examined):
Infiltrating adenocarcinoma present. Predominantly Gleason pattern 4 with cribriform architecture and poorly-formed glands. Focal Gleason pattern 5 (single cells) is present at the infiltrating edge. Estimated tumor involvement: approximately 65-75% of core K; 60-70% of core L. Perineural invasion identified in core K. Rare benign glands present.

Gleason score (cores K-L): 4+5=9, Grade Group 5

------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES / IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY
------------------------------------------------------------------------

Immunohistochemical (IHC) stains were performed on selected sections from cores A and E where diagnostic confirmation and subtype characterization were requested. Results are as follows:

Markersfor prostatic lineage and adenocarcinoma confirmation:
  PSA (Prostate Specific Antigen):
    Result: POSITIVE - strong, diffuse cytoplasmic and luminal staining in tumor cells
  PSMA (Prostate-Specific Membrane Antigen):
    Result: POSITIVE - strong membranous staining, 3+ intensity in the majority of tumor cells (estimated >90% of cells)
  NKX3.1:
    Result: POSITIVE - strong nuclear expression in tumor cells, confirming prostatic origin
  AR (Androgen Receptor):
    Result: POSITIVE - strong nuclear staining, estimated >95% of tumor nuclei

Basal cell/high-grade PIN markers:
  CK5/6 (high molecular weight cytokeratin):
    Result: NEGATIVE in tumor cells; positive internal control (benign basal cells present in adjacent non-neoplastic glands)
  p63:
    Result: NEGATIVE in tumor cells; positive in basal layer of adjacent benign glands
  HMWCK (34betaE12):
    Result: NEGATIVE in tumor cells
    Interpretation: Absence of basal cell layer in tumor glands consistent with invasive carcinoma

Neuroendocrine differentiation markers (performed given high-grade morphology):
  Synaptophysin:
    Result: NEGATIVE
  Chromogranin A:
    Result: NEGATIVE
  CD56:
    Result: NEGATIVE
    Interpretation: No evidence of small cell/neuroendocrine differentiation at this time

Proliferation index:
  Ki-67 (MIB-1):
    Result: Approximately 30-35% nuclear positivity in tumor cells (high proliferative activity, consistent with high-grade adenocarcinoma)

Note: MSI/MMR IHC and PD-L1 assessment deferred to molecular/NGS testing platform per ordering physician request.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS
------------------------------------------------------------------------

** PROSTATE GLAND, TRANSRECTAL ULTRASOUND-GUIDED BIOPSY (12 CORES), BILATERAL SYSTEMATIC SAMPLING:

   HIGH-GRADE PROSTATIC ADENOCARCINOMA (ACINAR TYPE)
   Gleason Score 9 (4+5), Grade Group 5 (ISUP 2014/WHO 2016)

   All 12 cores positive for adenocarcinoma (12/12)
   Bilateral involvement (right and left lobes, apex to base)
   Tumor involvement ranges from approximately 40% to 100% of individual core length

   PERINEURAL INVASION: PRESENT (identified in cores A, C, H, I, K)
   LYMPHOVASCULAR INVASION: NOT IDENTIFIED
   NEUROENDOCRINE DIFFERENTIATION: NOT PRESENT (IHC negative)
   EXTRAPROSTATIC EXTENSION: Cannot be assessed on needle core biopsy

Gleason Grading Summary by Region:
   Right base/mid (A-D): Gleason 9 (4+5), Grade Group 5
   Left base/mid (E-H):  Gleason 8 (4+4), Grade Group 4
   Bilateral apex (I-L): Gleason 9 (4+5), Grade Group 5

Overall (global) Gleason score: 9 (4+5), Grade Group 5

------------------------------------------------------------------------
COMMENT
------------------------------------------------------------------------

The morphology and immunoprofile (PSA+, NKX3.1+, PSMA+, AR+, CK5/6-, synaptophysin-, chromogranin-) are consistent with high-grade acinar prostatic adenocarcinoma. The absence of neuroendocrine markers is noted; clinical correlation with serum chromogranin A is suggested if clinically indicated. The high Ki-67 proliferation index (30-35%) is in keeping with the Grade Group 5 designation. No small cell or treatment-related neuroendocrine transdifferentiation is identified in this diagnostic specimen. Molecular/genomic testing (NGS panel and germline assessment) has been requested separately.

Pathologist: [Attending Pathologist, MD]
Date Signed: [DATE]
Case reviewed and confirmed by: [Senior Pathologist, MD]
